result.summary: Neon exposes a REST API, a hosted MCP server, and a CLI; all require authentication via Neon-issued API keys and/or hosted OAuth for MCP/CLI login.
result.description: Neon provides a managed Postgres backend platform with branching, compute endpoints, and related services for applications and AI agents. Its product includes Neon Postgres plus services such as a Data API, auth, functions, storage, and AI tooling.

neon_api_key.label: Neon API key
neon_api_key.setup: In the [Neon API reference](https://api-docs.neon.tech/reference/createapikey), use the API key endpoints or create one via the Neon setup flows such as `npx neon@latest init` described in [Connect MCP clients to Neon](https://neon.com/docs/ai/connect-mcp-clients-to-neon). The CLI auth docs say the CLI resolves authentication from `--api-key` first, then `NEON_API_KEY`, so this is the reusable developer credential for API and CLI access.

neon_mcp_oauth.label: Neon MCP OAuth client/session
neon_mcp_oauth.setup: Use an MCP client that supports OAuth against Neon's hosted MCP server at [mcp.neon.tech](https://mcp.neon.tech/mcp). The server advertises OAuth metadata at [authorization server metadata](https://mcp.neon.tech/.well-known/oauth-authorization-server), including dynamic client registration. Neon docs for [Connect MCP clients to Neon](https://neon.com/docs/ai/connect-mcp-clients-to-neon) describe authenticating MCP clients with Neon via OAuth.
